content.full_text:
Fern Porrrrcar- PnacrrcEs CouurssroN
428 J Street o Suite 620 . Sacramento, CA 95814-2329
(916) 322-5660 r Fax (916) 322-0886
December 4,2003
The Honorable Roger D. Randall, Retired
San Luis Obispo Superior Court
907 Mesa Street
Morro Bay, CA 93442
Re
Your Request for Advice
Our FiIe No. A-03-261
Dear Judge Randall:
This letter is in response to your request for advice regarding the statement of
economic interests filing provisions of the Political Reform Act (the ,.4ct,).I please
note, the Commission does not confirm verbal advice. Consequently, we keat your
request as a new one for written advice.
QUESTION
As a recently retired judge who expects to continue to sit by assignment, are you
required to file a leaving office statement of economic interests (Form ZOO; or shoutd you
continue filing annual statements?
CONCLUSION
Because you have not filed a leaving office statement for the superior court and
you expect to serve at least 30 days during 2004, you must continue filing annual
statements of economic interests. Your statement must be filed with the clerk of the court
to which you are assigned.
FACTS
You retired as a superior court judge on November 4,2003. You intend to
continue to sit by assignment through the judicial assignments program operated by the
chiefjustice through the Adminiskative Office of the Courts. You indicated that you
I Government Code sections 81000 - gl}l4. Commission regulations appear at Title 2, sections
18109-18997, of the California Code of Regulations.
'=---:'


File No. A-03-261
Page No. 2
anticipate serving at least 30 days during 2004 in our telephone conversation of
November 4,2003.
ANALYSIS
The Act requires judges, including retired judges who continue to serye on a
part-time basis, to file statements of economic interests (Form 700). (Sections 87200-
87204.) However, regulation 18724 provides that retired judges are not required to file
statements of economic interests unless they serve or expect to serve 30 days or more
in a calendar yeff on assignment, pursuant to Article VI, Section 6 of the ialifornia
Constitution. Thus, even though you have retired, since you expect to serve at least 30
days in calendar year 2004, you are not required to file a leaving office statement, but
must continue to file annual statements of economic interests.
If you have any questions or need further assistance, please contact me at (916)
322-5660.
Sincerely,
Luisa Menchaca
By:
PhD
Political Reform Consultant II
Technical Assistance Division
KSMjg
